prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a black screen; no visible objects or people are discernible in the first sampled frame.

By the middle of the clip, the scene has changed to a dim studio-like setting with a plain dark gray background. Two adult men are visible. The man on the left, wearing a light-colored collared shirt, faces forward and appears to be speaking or reacting, with one hand slightly raised. The man on the right, partly in profile, holds a large shoulder-style video camera/camcorder up near his face and points it toward the man on the left, as if recording him.

The final frame is a product display shot. A black camcorder is centered against a dark gradient background. Large white text above it reads “MAGNAVOX,” and smaller text underneath reads “Smart. Very smart.” The camcorder itself shows visible branding and labeling, including “VIDEO ESCORT” and “MAGNAVOX.” The product is presented cleanly and prominently, with no people visible in this final shot.
